name: Immutable Baseline Protocol (PR-F2, handover F-004)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-004. Author ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md: the reusable immutable-baseline protocol. Per .agent/feature_list_005.json feature 034 and product_spec_005.md FR-03, a baseline is a point-in-time, immutable snapshot spanning six reference categories -- framework, repository, dependency, CI, planning, and evidence references -- with explicit fields and evidence rules, and the protocol MUST make revision-pinning explicit: every evidence item is anchored to a declared repository revision/SHA and a timestamp, and a baseline MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions (a defined FAIL condition). Per docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md's Artifact Locations section, the machine-readable baseline artifact convention is `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json`. This feature is protocol-authoring only: no schema (037 owns schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, and depends on THIS feature so it cannot yet be in flight), no tooling, no capability routing or module-index prose beyond the single Module Navigation table-row flip this feature's own deliverable requires (ecosystem/README.md's '02' row, from feature 032's index; see the concurrency_fence and 038-tolerance design_notes below for why a wholesale schema/tools non-goal check is not used here).
design_notes.planned_document_structure: ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md uses the same house pattern as the shipped ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (numbered '## N. Title' sections, mirroring methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md): '## 1. Overview', '## 2. When to Capture', '## 3. Baseline Fields' (states the six reference categories -- framework, repository, dependency, CI, planning, evidence -- realizing 'Baseline fields ... are defined'), '## 4. Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule' (states 'revision' and 'timestamp' literally, and the literal phrase 'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions' plus the token 'FAIL condition' -- realizing 'Baseline cannot mix evidence from unspecified revisions' as an explicit, defined FAIL condition, not merely implied), '## 5. Immutability Rule' (states both 'immutable' and 'point-in-time' literally; a baseline is never edited in place, only superseded by a later baseline), '## 6. Baseline Artifact' (states the literal path convention `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json`, and a forward prose reference to schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json as not-yet-present, matching 01_clean_state_preflight.md Section 7's precedent for schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json), '## 7. References' (points back to the NIP, forward to ecosystem/README.md and methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md, and to 01_clean_state_preflight.md as the protocol this one follows in the lifecycle). Frontmatter: id 'nizam-ecosystem-evidence-baseline', status 'active', version '0.1.0' (first cut, matching 01_clean_state_preflight.md's own 0.1.0), authoritative_source 'ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md' (self-referential house pattern). This structure is committed to here because the anchored, section-scoped verification entries below reference these exact heading strings verbatim; the generator's implementation step MUST use them verbatim or the section-anchored verification commands will legitimately fail (a heading-text mismatch is a defect to fix, per Circuit Breaker attempt 1, not a scope violation). A companion one-line edit to ecosystem/README.md's Module Navigation table flips only the '| 02 | ... | Planned |' row to '| 02 | ... | Shipped |' -- the table itself and its Shipped/Planned convention were authored by feature 032, so this feature edits exactly that one pre-existing cell and nothing else in the file.
design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof: Before proposing the checks below, the section-scoped primitive (tools/verify_lib.sh vlib_section_grep, the same anti-vacuity-approved primitive contracts 031 and 033 used) was dry-run against two throwaway fixtures built and destroyed in the scratchpad (never committed, no repo-tree side effect, confirmed via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after): a GOOD fixture matching the planned structure verbatim, and a BAD fixture that keeps most of the structure but omits the 'evidence' category token in Section 3, the literal 'timestamp' / 'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions' / 'FAIL condition' phrases in Section 4, the literal 'immutable' token in Section 5, and the literal artifact path in Section 6. Result: all 11 positive assertions (six category tokens in Sec 3; 'revision'/'timestamp'/'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions'/'FAIL condition' in Sec 4; 'immutable'/'point-in-time' in Sec 5; the literal artifact path in Sec 6) returned exit 0 against GOOD; the corresponding BAD-fixture negative probes (missing 'evidence' token, missing 'timestamp', missing the 'MUST NOT mix' phrase, missing 'FAIL condition', missing 'immutable', missing the artifact path) each independently returned exit 1, proving each check is discriminating and non-vacuous, not merely satisfied by any plausible-looking document. REVISION 1 correction (per validator Mode A round-0 rejection): the round-0 draft of the README one-line-flip check (verification entry 9) used a fixed-string pattern with an erroneous backslash before each backtick (`'| 02 | \`02_evidence_baseline.md\` | Baseline | Shipped |'`); inside single quotes, bash does not interpret that backslash as an escape, so the pattern literally required a backslash character immediately before each backtick -- a byte sequence the real README (plain backticks, feature 032's authored table) never contains. The validator proved this live: a scratch copy with the row genuinely, correctly flipped to Shipped still produced NO MATCH under the round-0 pattern. This revision removes the stray backslashes (entry 9 now reads `'| 02 | `02_evidence_baseline.md` | Baseline | Shipped |'`, plain backticks, no escaping needed inside single quotes). The corrected check was then re-dry-run, verbatim as extracted from this contract's own JSON (not retyped by hand), against a genuine before/after scratch pair of the real ecosystem/README.md: run against the unmodified real file it FAILs ('ecosystem/README.md 02 row not flipped to Shipped', exit 1) exactly as expected pre-implementation; run against a scratch copy produced by a `sed` substitution that changes ONLY the 02 row's last cell (Planned -> Shipped, confirmed via `diff -u` showing exactly one changed line, all seven other Module Navigation rows byte-identical) it PASSes (exit 0). This is the genuine, empirically re-verified before/after proof superseding the round-0 claim. Separately, `git diff --no-index --numstat` between the two scratch copies (verification entry 10's proof) reports exactly '1\t1' (one line changed) -- proving the numstat-scoped one-line-edit guard (a distinct check from entry 9) is calibrated correctly; this numstat proof was unaffected by the entry-9 backslash defect and was not disputed by the validator. The per-file '00/03-08' non-goal loop (see non_goals and verification entry for the ls-false-pass fix precedent) was dry-run against a scratch simulated ecosystem/ directory (never the real one): clean state PASSes, creating exactly one simulated forbidden file FAILs and names it, removing it restores PASS -- proving the loop discriminates a single stray file, which is the exact defect contract 033's rev1 fixed in the ls-based form. All other (non-content) checks -- scope guard, .agent immutability guard, schema/tools non-goal exclusions, planning-doc non-goal, validate.sh green -- were additionally dry-run directly against the real, unmodified working tree (see the Generator dispatch's dry-run table) and currently PASS, as expected of regression-style guards in a clean tree; only the content-anchored checks against the not-yet-created ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md itself, and the README one-line-flip check, are expected to FAIL now and PASS after implementation.
design_notes.concurrency_fence: Feature 038 (Preflight Verdict Schema + Fixtures) runs CONCURRENTLY in this same working tree, with contracted surfaces schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json (new), schema/README.md (possible index update), fixture files under tools/fixtures/, .agent/contracts/038.json, and .agent/evidence/038*/.agent/qa/038* -- none of which this feature touches. This feature's scope-guard (verification entry 10) allow-lists schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md, and the tools/fixtures/ prefix SOLELY to tolerate 038's concurrent legitimate edits without a false-positive scope-guard failure -- exactly as contract 033 allow-listed feature 032's concurrent ecosystem/README.md/NIZAM.json edits. Note feature 037 (Baseline Schema + Fixtures, which WOULD touch schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json) depends on THIS feature (034) per product_spec_005.md Sec 7's execution order, so 037 cannot be in flight concurrently with 034 and is NOT tolerated here; verification entry 13 positively asserts schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json does not yet exist, which would catch 037 running out of dependency order as a side effect. Because 038 may legitimately change schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md, and tools/fixtures/* during this same window, the non-goal check for 'the rest of schema/ and tools/' (verification entry 14) is NOT a blind byte-identical-to-HEAD assertion over the whole directories (that pattern, used by contract 033 when no other feature was concurrently touching those directories, would produce a false failure against 038's own legitimate work here); instead it uses git pathspec excludes (`':(exclude)schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'`, `':(exclude)schema/README.md'`, `':(exclude)tools/fixtures'`) so everything else under schema/ and tools/ remains asserted byte-identical to HEAD while 038's declared surfaces are structurally excluded from the assertion, not merely allow-listed by prose.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edits, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happen under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally. Verification entry 11 (the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard) mirrors the precedent in contracts 025/028/031/033 exactly: it excludes only this feature's own new 034.json contract and 034*.json qa/review artifacts, plus feature 038's own concurrently-authored 038.json contract and 038*.json qa/review artifacts (038 is a different, independently-scoped in-flight feature, not a historical/immutable one -- same reasoning contract 033 applied to feature 032). It deliberately does NOT check .agent/run_state.json or any feature_list_00N.json (the orchestrator's own routinely-and-legitimately-modified concern).

files_create.purpose: The reusable immutable-baseline protocol: a point-in-time, immutable snapshot over framework, repository, dependency, CI, planning, and evidence references, with explicit baseline fields (six reference categories) and the explicit revision/timestamp anchoring rule -- a baseline MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions, a defined FAIL condition -- plus the machine-readable verdict artifact convention `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json`. UDS frontmatter per the house pattern (see design_notes.planned_document_structure).

files_modify.purpose: One-line Module Navigation table update: flip the pre-existing '02' row (`02_evidence_baseline.md` / Baseline) from 'Planned' to 'Shipped' now that this feature lands the file. No other edit to this file (table structure, other rows, and all other prose are feature 032's authored content and are left byte-for-byte intact except this one cell).

non_goals: No creation or edit of schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json or any other schema/*.json file except the tolerated concurrent feature 038 surfaces (schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md) -- the machine-readable baseline schema is feature 037's scope (037 depends on this feature and cannot be in flight concurrently; verification entry 13 positively asserts the baseline schema file does not yet exist)

No creation or edit of any file under tools/ except tolerating feature 038's concurrent tools/fixtures/* additions -- deterministic tooling is features 041/042's scope; this feature authors protocol prose only
No edit to NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, or tools/SKILL.md -- capability routing is feature 040's scope

No creation or edit of any other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, 03_engineering_audit.md, 04_dependency_reconciliation.md, 05_release_train_coordination.md, 06_simplification_review.md, 07_progress_comparison.md, 08_ga_gate.md) -- those belong to features 035, 036, and successor-phase-deferred protocols per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3

No edit to ecosystem/README.md beyond the single '02' row Shipped/Planned cell -- the table structure, the Canonical Lifecycle prose, the Capability Routing section, and the Consumer Convention section are feature 032's authored content and stay byte-for-byte intact

No edit to ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (feature 033's shipped deliverable) or CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- no planning-artifact or debt changes are authorized by this feature

No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 034.json, any .agent/qa/*.json, or any feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator
No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false

verification.acceptance_test: F-004/AT: the protocol doc file exists.
verification.command: test -f ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity master proof, mirroring the 031/033 fixture-probe precedent): ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md does not exist at HEAD, so every content-anchored check below (entries 3-8) is proven non-vacuous by construction -- nothing at HEAD can satisfy them because the target artifact itself does not yet exist there.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md already exists at HEAD -- content checks below would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter invalid"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (house-pattern frontmatter fields): id is 'nizam-ecosystem-evidence-baseline', status is 'active', and authoritative_source is self-referential.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=open('ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); ok=(fm.get('id')=='nizam-ecosystem-evidence-baseline' and fm.get('status')=='active' and fm.get('authoritative_source')=='ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md'); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter house-pattern fields incorrect"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-004/AT 'Baseline fields ... are defined' (STRENGTHENED from feature_list_005.json's coarse whole-file check per the anti-vacuity mandate -- section-anchored, not vacuous): the '## 3. Baseline Fields' section names all six reference categories: framework, repository, dependency, CI, planning, and evidence.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; for tok in framework repository dependency 'CI' planning evidence; do vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 3\. Baseline Fields$' "$tok" || { echo "Baseline Fields section missing category token: $tok"; exit 1; }; done; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-004/AT 'Baseline cannot mix evidence from unspecified revisions' (STRENGTHENED, section-anchored): the '## 4. Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule' section states 'revision', 'timestamp', the literal phrase 'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions', and 'FAIL condition' -- making the revision-pinning rule and its FAIL consequence explicit, not merely implied.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 4\. Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule$' 'revision' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 4\. Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule$' 'timestamp' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 4\. Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule$' 'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 4\. Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule$' 'FAIL condition' && echo PASS || { echo "Revision and Timestamp Anchoring Rule section missing one of: revision / timestamp / 'MUST NOT mix evidence from unspecified revisions' / 'FAIL condition'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (immutability, point-in-time nature, per NIP-0001's 'immutable, machine-readable evidence baselines' goal): the '## 5. Immutability Rule' section states both 'immutable' and 'point-in-time' literally.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 5\. Immutability Rule$' 'immutable' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 5\. Immutability Rule$' 'point-in-time' && echo PASS || { echo "Immutability Rule section missing 'immutable' or 'point-in-time'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (machine-readable baseline artifact convention, per the NIP's 'Artifact Locations' section): the '## 6. Baseline Artifact' section states the literal path convention `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json`.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md '^## 6\. Baseline Artifact$' '\.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline\.json' && echo PASS || { echo "Baseline Artifact section missing the .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/baseline.json convention"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (README one-line flip, scoped to exactly the '02' row): ecosystem/README.md's Module Navigation table now marks the 02 row 'Shipped'.
verification.command: grep -Fq '| 02 | `02_evidence_baseline.md` | Baseline | Shipped |' ecosystem/README.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md 02 row not flipped to Shipped"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (README one-line-only edit guard, dry-run proven against before/after scratch copies): ecosystem/README.md's diff against HEAD is exactly one inserted and one deleted line.
verification.command: out=$(git diff --numstat HEAD -- ecosystem/README.md); [ "$out" = "$(printf '1\t1\tecosystem/README.md')" ] && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/README.md diff is not exactly 1 insertion + 1 deletion: [$out]"; exit 1; }
bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (scope guard, untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant per design_notes.concurrency_fence -- schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/README.md, and tools/fixtures/ are feature 038's concurrently in-flight scope, and v0.6.0-release-notes.md is a pre-existing untracked artifact this feature does not touch): only this feature's own new/modified files, plus the tolerated concurrent/pre-existing paths, changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md' 'ecosystem/README.md' 'schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' 'schema/README.md' 'tools/fixtures/' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (.agent/ immutability guard, mirroring the precedent in contracts 025/028/031/033, extended per design_notes.concurrency_fence to tolerate feature 038's concurrently in-flight contract/qa artifacts): no historical (pre-existing, already-approved) .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact was edited -- excludes only this feature's own new 034.json contract and 034*.json qa/review artifacts, AND feature 038's own concurrently-authored 038.json contract and 038*.json qa/review artifacts (038 is a different, independently-scoped in-flight feature, not a historical/immutable one).
verification.command: changed=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts ':(exclude).agent/contracts/034.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/034*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/038.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/038*.json'); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo 'immutable audit record(s) touched:'; echo "$changed"; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, dependency-order guard per design_notes.concurrency_fence): schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json (feature 037's deliverable, which depends on THIS feature) does not yet exist -- this feature performs zero schema work of its own, and this also structurally catches 037 running out of dependency order.
verification.command: test -f schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json && { echo "schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json already exists -- out of dependency order or out of this feature's scope"; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, concurrency-tolerant via git pathspec excludes rather than a blind whole-directory assertion, per design_notes.concurrency_fence): every schema/*.json file except feature 038's declared schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json and schema/README.md, and every file under tools/ except feature 038's declared tools/fixtures/, are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ tools/ ':(exclude)schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' ':(exclude)schema/README.md' ':(exclude)tools/fixtures' && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope schema/tooling file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ tools/ ':(exclude)schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json' ':(exclude)schema/README.md' ':(exclude)tools/fixtures'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, and docs/planning/DEBT.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope planning document was modified"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, per-file existence loop per contract 033 rev1's ls-false-pass fix -- used correctly from the start here, not a repeated defect): no other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00, 03-08) was created.
verification.command: bad=0; for f in ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md; do if [ -e "$f" ]; then echo "out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md (feature 033's shipped deliverable) and CHANGELOG.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md CHANGELOG.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope file (01_clean_state_preflight.md or CHANGELOG.md) was modified"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/034-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/034-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/034-verify-18.txt for the eighteenth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 18 entries). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

findings: BLOCKER (verification[8]/entry 9): the README Shipped-flip fixed-string pattern contained an erroneous backslash before each backtick inside single quotes (bash does not treat backslash as an escape character within single quotes), so the pattern required a literal backslash byte the real, correctly-flipped README never contains -- the only positive check of the files_modify deliverable could never pass genuine correct work. Reproduced live by the validator against a scratch copy with the row genuinely flipped: NO MATCH.

MAJOR (design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof): the round-0 claim that the flip check 'passes against a copy with only that row's last cell changed' was empirically false under the round-0 pattern.
round0_rejection.fixed_in: revision 1 (this document): entry 9's command rewritten with plain backticks (no backslash); design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof restated truthfully from a genuine re-run after the fix, verbatim-extracted from this contract's own JSON against real before/after scratch copies of ecosystem/README.md (FAIL against the unmodified Planned row, PASS against a scratch copy with only that cell changed to Shipped, diff-confirmed to be exactly one changed line).
